main_event: Bright light with touch of red observed nightly between 1900-2000 hours over Lake Erie, 70 miles east of Windsor, Ontario, for two weeks prior to 15 November 1965; RCAF investigated and concluded a plane had crashed in the area.
sighted_object: bright light with a touch of red; seemed to glow up, dim out, move slightly horizontally and downward, then disappear
location: Lake Erie, Ontario, Canada (70 mi E of Windsor, 15 mi W of Cedar Springs, Ont.)
date: November 1965 (approximately two weeks prior to 15 Nov 1965)
witness_description: Numerous individuals from area along Lake Erie; reported by Lt Logemann from Selfridge AFB after inquiry from Chatham (Ont.) Daily News reporter
interesting_points: No ATIC Form 329 record card — file stamped 'No Case Info Only'
RCAF investigated initial sightings and concluded a plane had crashed in the area
Lt Logman from Selfridge AFB called 15 Nov 1965 to ask if flares had been dropped; Sgt Moody said nothing could be done about Canadian reports
Observed nightly between 1900 and 2000 hours; each evening the object was seen was extremely clear
No formal ATIC conclusion; case filed as informational only
